CPet::ParseCommand.notes: COutPacket(0xB5)+EncodeBuffer(petLockerSN,8)+Encode1(commandWithName)+Encode1(command) (verified live v87 @0x748a35). Corrected: the prior baseline traced a phantom 4th Encode1 'success flag' that the client never sends — the real send is exactly 3 fields, matching Atlas command.go [WriteLong, WriteBool, WriteByte].

CWvsContext::OnFriendResult.note: Dispatcher — reads Decode1(mode) then switches. Wire shape identical to v83/v95 for all sub-modes.

CWvsContext::OnFriendResult#ListUpdate.note: mode=7/10/18 (LOADED/LIST_UPDATE/RESET). Calls CFriend::Reset(v2) — reads the full friend-list wire from the packet. Wire identical to v83.

CWvsContext::OnFriendResult#Update.note: mode=8. Calls sub_AD8963 — wire identical to v83.

CWvsContext::OnFriendResult#Invite.note: mode=9 (INVITE). Reads characterId+name then sub_AD8963(friend-list). Wire identical to v83/v95.

CWvsContext::OnFriendResult#ChannelChang.note: mode=0x14. Reads characterId+channelId+mapId. Wire identical to v83/v95.

CWvsContext::OnFriendResult#CapacityUpda.note: mode=0x15. Stores Decode1(maxFriendCount). Wire identical to v83/v95.

CWvsContext::OnFriendResult#Error.note: mode=0x10/0x11/0x13/0x16. Conditionally reads Decode1(hasMsg)+DecodeStr(msg). Wire identical to v83/v95.

CWvsContext::OnMemoResult.note: Dispatcher — Decode1(mode)-3 dispatch: mode=3→Display (reads list of GW_Memo), mode=4→SendSuccess, mode=5→SendError variants, mode=7→Refresh. Wire shape identical to v83/v95.

CWvsContext::OnMemoResult#Display.note: mode=3. Reads Decode1(count) then GW_Memo::Decode per entry. Wire identical to v83/v95.

CWvsContext::OnMemoResult#SendSuccess.note: mode=4. No further reads. Wire identical to v83/v95.

CWvsContext::OnMemoResult#SendError.note: mode=5. Reads Decode1(errorCode). Wire identical to v83/v95.

CWvsContext::OnMemoResult#Refresh.note: mode=7 (value 3+4). No further reads beyond mode byte. Wire identical to v83/v95.

CField::SendChatMsg.note: Chat message send. Wire shape identical to v83/v95.

CUIMessenger::OnPacket.note: Dispatcher — Decode1(mode) then switches. Sub-modes: 0=OnEnter, 1=OnSelfEnterResult, 2=OnLeave, 3=OnInvite, 4=OnInviteResult, 5=OnBlocked, 6=OnChat, 7=OnAvatar, 8=OnMigrated. Wire shape identical to v83/v95.

CUIMessenger::OnPacket#InviteSent.note: mode=4 (OnInviteResult). Reads Decode1(result). Wire identical to v83/v95.

CUIMessenger::OnPacket#InviteDeclined.note: mode=5 (OnBlocked). Reads DecodeStr(characterName). Wire identical to v83/v95.

CUIMessenger::OnCreate.note: client accepts a messenger invite; COutPacket(0x80)+Encode1(0,ENTER)+Encode4(messengerId) (verified live v87 @0x8b62ed). Corrected: the prior baseline mislabeled this SERVERBOUND send as clientbound with a single Decode4 — it is the ENTER send, and the leading op byte is Atlas's messenger Operation wrapper (composed on the Atlas side by the audit).

CUIMessenger::OnDestroy.note: Messenger destroy — no dedicated function; messenger close handled by the mode=2 (Leave) path.

CUIMessenger::ProcessChat.note: Outbound chat in messenger — sends mode+message. Wire identical to v83/v95.

CUIMessenger::SendInviteMsg.note: Send invite request. Wire identical to v83/v95.

CWvsContext::OnPartyResult#OperationBody.note: Dispatcher — reads Decode1(mode) then dispatches. PARTYDATA size in v87: qmemcpy/memset with 0x12A (298 bytes), same as v83 (NOT v95's 0x17A=378). Gate v95plus (>=95) correctly excludes v87.

CWvsContext::OnPartyResult#Created.note: mode=4 (CREATE). v87 reads: partyId+name+jobId+level+autoJoin — IDA confirmed at 0xad6ed6-0xad6f06. Gate widened from v95plus to v84plus.

CWvsContext::OnPartyResult#Invite.note: mode=4 (same as Created in v87's opcode table). See Created.

CWvsContext::OnPartyResult#Join.note: mode=15 (JOIN). Reads partyLeaderId+inviteeName+PARTYDATA. PARTYDATA=298 bytes (0x12A) in v87.

CWvsContext::OnPartyResult#Update.note: mode=5 (UPDATE — v=5-4=1 path). Reads leaderId+PARTYDATA. PARTYDATA=298 bytes.

CWvsContext::OnPartyResult#Left.note: mode=12 (LEFT — v=12-4=8 path). Reads leaderId+PARTYDATA+discharge flag. PARTYDATA=298 bytes.

CWvsContext::OnPartyResult#Disband.note: mode=14 (DISBAND — v=14-4=10 path). Reads leaderId+PARTYDATA. PARTYDATA=298 bytes.

CWvsContext::OnPartyResult#Error.note: Various error modes (13, 16, 17, 18, 19). No further reads beyond mode byte.

CWvsContext::OnGuildResult.note: Dispatcher — reads Decode1(mode) then dispatches. Wire shapes below reflect v87 IDA at 0xacf7d3.

CWvsContext::OnGuildResult#MemberJoined.note: mode=39 (MEMBER_JOIN). Reads guildId+GUILDMEMBER::Decode. Wire identical to v83/v95.

CWvsContext::OnGuildResult#MemberLeft.note: mode=38 (MEMBER_LEAVE). Reads guildId+characterId. Wire identical to v83/v95.

CWvsContext::OnGuildResult#MemberExpel.note: mode=40 (MEMBER_EXPEL). Reads guildId+characterId. Wire identical to v83/v95.

CWvsContext::OnGuildResult#MemberStatusU.note: mode=48 (MEMBER_STATUS). Reads guildId+charId+channelId. Wire identical to v83/v95.

CWvsContext::OnGuildResult#MemberTitleUp.note: mode=54 (MEMBER_TITLE). Reads guildId+charId+titleIdx. Wire identical to v83/v95.

CWvsContext::OnGuildResult#NoticeChange.note: mode=44 (NOTICE). Reads guildId+notice. Wire identical to v83/v95.

CWvsContext::OnGuildResult#TitleChange.note: mode=46 (TITLE). Reads guildId+5 title strings. Wire identical to v83/v95.

CWvsContext::OnGuildResult#EmblemChange.note: mode=50 (EMBLEM). Reads guildId+emblem data. Wire identical to v83/v95.

CWvsContext::OnGuildResult#CapacityChang.note: mode=53 (CAPACITY). Reads guildId+maxMembers. Wire identical to v83/v95.

CWvsContext::OnGuildResult#Disband.note: mode=43 (DISBAND). Reads guildId. Wire identical to v83/v95.

CWvsContext::OnGuildResult#RequestAgreem.note: mode=3 (REQUEST_AGREEMENT). Reads guildId+name1+name2. Wire identical to v83/v95.

CWvsContext::OnGuildResult#AgreementResp.note: mode=37 (AGREEMENT). Reads guildId+characterId+bool+name. Wire identical to v83/v95.

CWvsContext::OnGuildResult#ErrorMessage.note: Various error modes. No further reads (or reads error code only). Wire identical to v83/v95.

CWvsContext::OnGuildResult#ErrorMessageW.note: Error mode with target name. Reads DecodeStr(characterName). Wire identical to v83/v95.

CMob::GenerateMovePath.notes: Opcode 0xC8. v87 wire shape matches v95 / JMS-v185 — the `(GMS && >83) || JMS` gates open the multiTargetForBall and randTimeForAreaAttack count+loop blocks, the hackedCodeCRC field between flyCtxTargetY and the Flush body, and the post-Flush bChasing/hasTarget/bChasing2/bChasingHack/tChaseDuration tail. Decompile cross-check at GMSv87_4GB.exe:0x6a6381 confirms COutPacket(0xC8) → Encode4 mobId → Encode2 ctrlSN+1 → Encode1 packed flags → Encode1 actionType → Encode4 skill m_Data → Encode4 multiTargetForBall count + 2×Encode4 per entry → Encode4 randTimeForAreaAttack count + 1×Encode4 per entry → Encode1 state → Encode4 hackedCode → Encode4 flyCtxTargetX → Encode4 flyCtxTargetY → Encode4 hackedCodeCRC → CMovePath::Flush → Encode1 bChasing → Encode1 hasTarget → Encode1 bChasing2 → Encode1 bChasingHack → Encode4 tChaseDuration.

calls.comment: flags byte (atlas dwFlag — multiBitFlag with nMoveType / bRiseByToss / etc.)

CStage::OnSetField.notes: SET_FIELD (clientbound, GMS v87). ENVELOPE-ONLY audit: the CharacterData blob (CharacterData::Decode @0x7c440a) is audited under the character domain (task-028) and is represented here by a single DecodeBuf boundary marker. v87 wire order (bCharacterData=1 branch): DecodeOpt(2, CClientOptMan::DecodeOpt @0x7c42c5) + channelId(4, Decode4 @0x7c42db) + sNotifierMessage(1) + bCharacterData(1) + nNotifierCheck(2) + [notifier string loop if !=0] + 3x seed(4) + CharacterData + OnSetLogoutGiftConfig(4x int32) + timestamp(8, DecodeBuffer p,8u). CROSS-VERSION (task-068 Phase 3 v87): v87 reads NO m_dwOldDriverID after channelId — sNotifierMessage (Decode1 @0x7c42fa) is read immediately after channelId(@0x7c42db), matching v83 @0x776020 and unlike v95 @0x71a0a0 which inserts an unconditional Decode4 there. Field introduced between v87 and v95. Atlas gate GMS>=95 CONFIRMED correct.

calls.comment: DecodeOpt (CClientOptMan::DecodeOpt @0x7c42c5) — atlas WriteShort(0) gated (GMS>83 || JMS); present v87
sNotifierMessage flag (HIBYTE, @0x7c42fa) — read immediately after channelId; v87 has NO m_dwOldDriverID Decode4 between (unlike v95)
nNotifierCheck (@0x7c431e; atlas writes 0 — no notifier title/content loop)
CharacterData::Decode (@0x7c440a) — ENVELOPE BOUNDARY; inner shape audited under character domain (task-028)
m_bPredictQuit (OnSetLogoutGiftConfig @0x7c4412) — atlas logout-gift int #1, gated (GMS>83 || JMS)

CStage::OnSetField#WarpToMap.notes: SET_FIELD warp path (clientbound, GMS v87). Same CStage::OnSetField handler @0x7c429c with bCharacterData=0 — the else branch (no CharacterData blob, @0x7c4423). v87 wire order: DecodeOpt(2) + channelId(4) + sNotifierMessage(1) + bCharacterData(1) + nNotifierCheck(2) + reviveFlag(1, @0x7c4423) + dwPosMap/mapId(4, @0x7c447b) + nPortal(1, @0x7c449d) + nHP(2, Decode2 @0x7c44a8) + bChaseEnable(1, @0x7c44c2) + [if chase: targetX(4)+targetY(4)] + timestamp(8). CROSS-VERSION (task-068 Phase 3 v87): (1) NO m_dwOldDriverID after channelId — matches v83, unlike v95. (2) nHP is Decode2 (2 bytes, @0x7c44a8 LOWORD), matching v83 @0x776020 and unlike v95 @0x71a0a0 which reads Decode4. nHP width widened between v87 and v95. Atlas nHP gate GMS>=95 (4 bytes) CONFIRMED correct.

calls.comment: DecodeOpt — atlas WriteShort(0) gated (GMS>83 || JMS); present v87
sNotifierMessage flag (@0x7c42fa; atlas writes 0) — no m_dwOldDriverID before it in v87
bCharacterData flag (@0x7c4307; atlas writes 0 — warp path)
revive flag (else branch @0x7c4423) — atlas WriteByte(0) gated (GMS>28 || JMS)
nHP — v87 Decode2 (2 bytes, LOWORD @0x7c44a8), matches v83; v95 reads Decode4. atlas WriteShort(2) for GMS<95

CField::OnFieldEffect#Summon.notes: FIELD_EFFECT case 0 (summon). CField::OnFieldEffect@0x55a910 case 0: Decode1(mode=0) + Decode1(effect v4 @0x55a948) + Decode4(x a2 @0x55a952) + Decode4(y v5 @0x55a95c). Maps atlas EffectSummon. v87 byte-identical to v95 (case 0 @0x53b790).

CField::OnFieldEffect#Tremble.notes: FIELD_EFFECT case 1 (tremble). CField::OnFieldEffect@0x55a910 case 1: Decode1(mode=1) + Decode1(bHeavyNShortTremble v18 @0x55abbb) + Decode4(delay v19 @0x55abbe) -> Effect_Tremble. Maps atlas EffectTremble. v87 byte-identical to v95 (case 1 @0x53bb74).

CField::OnFieldEffect#String.notes: FIELD_EFFECT cases 2 (object/SetObjectState @0x55a9fb), 3 (screen/ShowScreenEffect @0x55aa2a), 4 (sound/play_field_sound @0x55aa7b), 6 (BGM/PlayBGM @0x55ab0c). Each: Decode1(mode) + DecodeStr(name/path). Maps atlas EffectString. v87 byte-identical to v95 (cases @0x53b8b3).

CField::OnFieldEffect#BossHp.notes: FIELD_EFFECT case 5 (boss HP tag / ShowMobHPTag). CField::OnFieldEffect@0x55a910 case 5: Decode1(mode=5) + Decode4(monsterId v34 @0x55aac5) + Decode4(currentHp v40 @0x55aacf) + Decode4(maxHp a2 @0x55aad9) + Decode1(tagColor v14 @0x55aae5) + Decode1(tagBackgroundColor v15 @0x55aae7). Maps atlas EffectBossHp. v87 byte-identical to v95 (case 5 @0x53b9c1).

CField::OnFieldEffect#RewardRullet.notes: FIELD_EFFECT case 7 (reward roulette / Effect_RewardRullet). CField::OnFieldEffect@0x55a910 case 7: Decode1(mode=7) + Decode4(nRewardJobIdx v20 @0x55abea) + Decode4(nRewardPartIdx v21 @0x55abf3) + Decode4(nRewardLevIdx v22 @0x55abf5). Maps atlas EffectRewardRullet. v87 byte-identical to v95 (case 7 @0x53bba4).

CField::OnBlowWeather.notes: BLOW_WEATHER (GMS v87). CField::OnBlowWeather@0x55c953: Decode1(m_nBlowType v3 @0x55c967) + Decode4(itemId v5 @0x55c97f); DecodeStr(message @0x55c99a) ONLY in the else branch (itemId!=0 && m_nBlowType==0, gated by if(!v5 || this[162]) @0x55c98b). Atlas EffectWeather (BAD-FORM single struct): WriteBool(!active)=blowType + WriteInt(itemId) + if active: WriteAsciiString(message). v87 byte-identical to v95 (@0x5468f0); conditional message is a tool limitation (per-mode table in FieldEffectWeather.md).

calls.comment: message (only when itemId!=0 && m_nBlowType==0; start path, @0x55c99a)

CField::OnClock.notes: CLOCK (GMS v87). v87 has NO standalone CField::OnClock symbol — the clock-set handler is inlined as sub_55DA5F@0x55DA5F (3x CClock::SetTimer callers). Dispatches on a leading clockType byte (Decode1 v4 @0x55da76) to 5 arms: case 0 EventClock (Decode4 seconds @0x55dd6d), case 1 TownClock (3x Decode1 hour/min/sec @0x55dd40-4d), case 2 TimerClock (Decode4 seconds @0x55dd0f), case 3 EventTimerClock (Decode1 enable @0x55dac2 + Decode4 seconds @0x55dae6), case 0x64 CakePieEventTimerClock (Decode1 enable + Decode1 flag2 v9 @0x55dadd + Decode4 seconds @0x55dae6). Atlas Clock (BAD-FORM single struct) Encode switches on the same clockType. v87 ALL 5 ARMS byte-identical to v95 (CField::OnClock@0x531510). The flat analyzer cannot model the mutually-exclusive arms; export lists the EventTimerClock(case 3) shape as a representative — verdict is a TOOL LIMITATION (all 5 modes manually verified). See FieldClock.md.

calls.comment: flag1 (EventTimerClock enable byte; representative arm = case 3 @0x55dac2)

CField_ContiMove::OnContiState.notes: CONTI_STATE / field transport state (clientbound, GMS v87). CField_ContiMove::OnContiState@0x577c21: Decode1(nState v3 @0x577c32) + Decode1(overrideAppear v4 @0x577c35). Switched: 0/1/6 EnterShipMove, 2/5 LeaveShipMove, 3/4 AppearShip (checks v4==1). Matches atlas Transport: WriteByte(state) + WriteBool(overrideAppear). v87 byte-identical to v95 (@0x54d5a0).

calls.comment: overrideAppear flag (v4; checked v4==1 in AppearShip case, @0x577c35)

CField::SendTransferFieldRequest.notes: CHANGE_MAP (GMS v87, COutPacket @0x557b97). Client field-transfer request. fieldKey=get_field()->m_bFieldKey @0x557bad. x/y (Encode2 each) emitted only when arg4(sPortal)!=NULL (test @0x557bf4); real callers pass a portal name so x/y travels WITH a non-empty portalName, while the null-portal Revive path sends an empty portalName and no x/y. chase = global dword_CA02A0 @0x557c50 (always present in v87); targetX/targetY only when chase. v87 byte-identical to v95 (@0x5345c0).

CUserLocal::CheckPortal_Collision.notes: CHANGE_MAP_SPECIAL (GMS v87). Client portal-script-enter request. Build site @0x9c88bb (type-9 script portal, COutPacket 0x67). fieldKey=get_field()+328 @0x9c88ce. v87 byte-identical to v95 (@0x919a10).

CAffectedAreaPool::OnAffectedAreaCreated.notes: SPAWN_MIST (clientbound, GMS v87). v87 layout (8 fields): Decode4(dwId v89 @0x432f6e) + Decode4(nType v91[0] @0x432f78) + Decode4(dwOwnerId v4 @0x432f82) + Decode4(nSkillID a2 @0x432f8b) + Decode1(nSLV Value @0x432f98) + Decode2(phase/delay v87 @0x432fa3) + DecodeBuffer(16, rcArea RECT @0x432fae) + Decode4(tEnd/phase Skill @0x432fba). CROSS-VERSION (task-068 Phase 3 v87): v87 reads ONLY ONE trailing Decode4 after the 16-byte RECT (no tStart), MATCHING v83 @0x431a63; v95 @0x437ec0 adds a leading tStart int32 (2 trailing Decode4s). Atlas matches NEITHER version (atlas writes v83-era origin int16s). STRUCTURAL DEFERRAL — see _pending.md AFFECTEDAREA-create-shape; NOT rewritten in this pass.

calls.comment: nType (mist type; ==3 is item-area-buff branch, v91[0] @0x432f78)
rcArea RECT (16 bytes = ltX,ltY,rbX,rbY as 4x int32, @0x432fae)
tEnd/nPhase (end time, Skill @0x432fba) — v87 has NO leading tStart (matches v83; v95 adds tStart)

CAffectedAreaPool::OnAffectedAreaRemoved.notes: REMOVE_MIST (clientbound, GMS v87). OnAffectedAreaRemoved@0x43388c reads a single Decode4 (affected-area id, v37 @0x4338ae) then despawns; matches atlas. v87 byte-identical to v95 (@0x4360a0).

CMessageBoxPool::OnMessageBoxEnterField.notes: SPAWN_KITE (clientbound, GMS v87). Kite = MessageBox field object. Decode4(dwMessageBoxID v58 @0x694e6f) + Decode4(nItemID +56 @0x694eb2) + DecodeStr(sMsg +8 @0x694ebb) + DecodeStr(sCharacterName +12 @0x694eea) + Decode2(ptMessageBox.x +28 @0x694f20) + Decode2(nType +32 @0x694f30). v87 byte-identical to v95 (@0x6369c0).

CMessageBoxPool::OnMessageBoxLeaveField.notes: REMOVE_KITE (clientbound, GMS v87). Reads animation flag then id: Decode1(bAnimation v1 @0x69546f) + Decode4(dwID v36 @0x69547b). v87 byte-identical to v95 (@0x635d60).

calls.comment: bAnimation (leave animation type; 0 = play despawn animation, v1 @0x69546f)

CMessageBoxPool::OnCreateFailed.notes: CANNOT_SPAWN_KITE (clientbound, GMS v87). Empty body — client reads nothing and shows a StringPool notice (485 @0x694e38). v87 byte-identical to v95 (@0x636760, empty body).

CNpcPool::OnNpcEnterField.notes: SPAWN_NPC. v87 CNpcPool::OnNpcEnterField@0x716fd5 reads Decode4(npcId) then (new-entry path) Decode4(templateId), then CNpc::Init@0x70db47 reads Decode2(x@0x70db7b)+Decode2(cy@0x70db89)+Decode1(moveAction@0x70dbb1)+Decode2(fh@0x70dbc1 via GetFoothold)+Decode2(rx0@0x70dbe1)+Decode2(rx1@0x70dbef)+Decode1(enabled@0x70dc84). Atlas Spawn writes Int(id)+Int(template)+Int16(x)+Int16(cy)+Byte(f)+Short(fh)+Int16(rx0)+Int16(rx1)+Byte(1). Field-for-field match — identical to v83/v95.

CNpcPool::OnNpcChangeController.notes: SPAWN_NPC_REQUEST_CONTROLLER. v87 CNpcPool::OnNpcChangeController@0x7170c8 reads Decode1(bLocal @0x7170da)+Decode4(npcId @0x7170dd); local (bLocal!=0) -> SetLocalNpc@0x716b88 which on the new-controller path Decode4(templateId @0x716be2) then CNpc::Init@0x70db47 reads the full spawn body Decode2(x)+Decode2(cy)+Decode1(moveAction)+Decode2(fh via GetFoothold)+Decode2(rx0)+Decode2(rx1)+Decode1(enabled). Atlas SpawnRequestController writes Byte(localFlag)+Int(npcId)+Int(templateId)+Int16(x)+Int16(cy)+Byte(f)+Short(fh)+Int16(rx0)+Int16(rx1)+Byte(enabled). Field-for-field match — identical to v95 (the remote arm SetRemoteNpc reads no spawn body; analyzer follows the local arm).

calls.comment: bLocal / v3 (1=local controller w/ spawn body, 0=remote)

CNpc::OnMove.notes: NPC_ACTION. v87 dispatched via CNpcPool::OnNpcPacket@0x716f20 which Decode4(npcId) BEFORE calling CNpc::OnMove@0x7101b0 (case 0x115; opcode shifted from v83 0x104 / handled at CSV/template layer). OnMove reads Decode1(action @0x7101c5)+Decode1(chatIdx @0x7101d3), then (template bMove gated) a CMovePath::OnMovePacket movement body. Movement presence is server-controlled in atlas (hasMovement) — conditional movement is a tool limitation. Export models the dispatcher-prefix int + the two action bytes. Matches v83/v95.

calls.comment: npcId (consumed by CNpcPool::OnNpcPacket@0x716f20 dispatcher before OnMove; atlas Action.objectId)

CUserLocal::OnTutorMsg#Message.notes: TALK_GUIDE. v87 CUserLocal::OnTutorMsg@0x9e36c9 has the SAME message/hint split as v83/v95: Decode1(bByMessage @0x9e36e6); the FALSE (==0) arm at 0x9e3714 calls DecodeStr(message)+Decode4(width)+Decode4(duration) -> CTutor::OnMessage(ZXString,int,int). Atlas GuideTalkMessage writes the leading bool as false. Phase-2 unconditional fix VERIFIED correct for v87 — no version gate needed (v83==v87==v95).

CUserLocal::OnTutorMsg#Idx.notes: TALK_GUIDE. Same v87 CUserLocal::OnTutorMsg@0x9e36c9 handler; the TRUE (!=0) arm at 0x9e36f8 calls Decode4(hintId)+Decode4(duration) -> CTutor::OnMessage(int,int), NO string. Same polarity as v83/v95. Atlas GuideTalkIdx writes the leading bool as true. Phase-2 unconditional fix VERIFIED correct for v87 — no version gate needed.

CScriptMan::OnScriptMessage.notes: SCRIPT_MESSAGE / NPC_TALK. v87 CScriptMan::OnScriptMessage@0x791666 reads the common header Decode1(speakerType)+Decode4(npcTemplate)+Decode1(msgType)+Decode1(bParam), then switch(msgType) to per-type handlers. NOTE: UNLIKE v83 (which had NO SayImage case), v87's switch HAS a SayImage case (case 1 -> OnSayImage), matching v95 — the dialog-type enum is the v95-style ordering: 0=Say,1=SayImage,2=AskYesNo,3=AskText,4=AskNumber,5=AskMenu,6=AskQuiz,7=AskSpeedQuiz,8=AskAvatar,9=AskMembershopAvatar,0xA=AskPet,0xB=AskPetAll,0xD=AskYesNoQuest,0xE=AskBoxText,0xF=AskSlideMenu. Atlas NpcConversation.Encode writes byte(speakerTypeId)+int(speakerTemplateId)+byte(msgType)+byte(param)+(param&4?int(secondaryNpcTemplateId))+WriteByteArray(detail); the msgType byte is resolved from tenant config (ResolveCode 'messageType'), so the per-version enum is handled at the template/config layer, NOT in the encoder. The param&4 secondary read and detail body are consumed inside each per-type handler (OnSay reads secondary first). Per-dialog-type bodies audited individually.

calls.comment: nMsgType (dialog-type discriminator; v87 enum = v95 ordering: 0=Say,1=SayImage,2=AskYesNo,3=AskText,4=AskNumber,5=AskMenu,6=AskQuiz,7=AskSpeedQuiz,8=AskAvatar,9=AskMembershopAvatar,10=AskPet,11=AskPetAll,13=AskYesNoQuest,14=AskBoxText,15=AskSlideMenu)
bParam (speaker flags; bit 2 (param&4) gates a secondary npc template id read inside the per-type handler)
secondaryNpcTemplateId (read at start of per-type body when bParam&4; e.g. v87 OnSay@0x79184a) -- guarded
per-type conversation detail body (opaque to wrapper; audited in NpcSay*/NpcAsk* reports) via WriteByteArray

CScriptMan::OnSay#Say.notes: Dialog type 0 (Say). v87 CScriptMan::OnSay@0x791828 body (after the param&4 secondary Decode4 at 0x79184a which the wrapper covers): DecodeStr(message @0x791853)+Decode1(bPrev @0x791869)+Decode1(bNext @0x79187b). Atlas SayConversationDetail: AsciiString(Message)+Bool(Previous)+Bool(Next). Match — identical to v83/v95.

CScriptMan::OnSayImage#SayImage.notes: Dialog type 1 (SayImage). v87 CScriptMan::OnSayImage@0x7919a9: Decode1(count @0x791a37)+loop count x DecodeStr(image @0x791a4d). Atlas SayImageConversationDetail (post-fix): Byte(len)+loop AsciiString. Count is a single byte (Decode1) NOT int — Phase-2 unconditional WriteByte fix VERIFIED correct for v87. NOTE: unlike v83 (no SayImage case in the OnScriptMessage switch), v87 IS reachable via the standard msgType dispatch (case 1), same as v95. Analyzer flattens the loop.

calls.comment: image path -- loop body (count iterations; analyzer flattens)

CScriptMan::OnAskYesNo#AskYesNo.notes: Dialog type 2 (AskYesNo; case 0xD AskYesNoQuest shares the same handler with a7=1). v87 CScriptMan::OnAskYesNo@0x791b70: DecodeStr(message @0x791b89) only. Atlas AskYesNoConversationDetail: AsciiString(Message). Match — identical to v83/v95.

CScriptMan::OnAskText#AskText.notes: Dialog type 3 (AskText). v87 CScriptMan::OnAskText@0x791cd0: DecodeStr(message @0x791ceb)+DecodeStr(default @0x791cfb)+Decode2(minLen @0x791d10)+Decode2(maxLen @0x791d22). Atlas AskTextConversationDetail: AsciiString(Message)+AsciiString(Default)+Int16(MinLength)+Int16(MaxLength). Match — identical to v83/v95.

CScriptMan::OnAskNumber#AskNumber.notes: Dialog type 4 (AskNumber). v87 CScriptMan::OnAskNumber@0x792020: DecodeStr(message @0x79203b)+Decode4(default @0x79204e)+Decode4(min @0x792058)+Decode4(max @0x79206a). Atlas AskNumberConversationDetail: AsciiString(Message)+Int(Default)+Int(Min)+Int(Max). Match — identical to v83/v95.

CScriptMan::OnAskMenu#AskMenu.notes: Dialog type 5 (AskMenu). v87 CScriptMan::OnAskMenu@0x7921a8: DecodeStr(message @0x7921c1) only (the menu options are embedded in the message string). Atlas AskMenuConversationDetail: AsciiString(Message). Match — identical to v83/v95.

CScriptMan::OnAskQuiz#AskQuiz.notes: Dialog type 6 (AskQuiz). v87 CScriptMan::OnAskQuiz@0x792b90 forwards to CWvsContext::OnInitialQuiz@0xabe75d: Decode1(flag @0xabe774); when flag==0 (show): DecodeStr(title @0xabe7bb)+DecodeStr(problem @0xabe7c9)+DecodeStr(hint @0xabe7d8)+Decode4(min @0xabe7ea)+Decode4(max @0xabe7f4)+Decode4(remainSec @0xabe808). Atlas AskQuizConversationDetail mirrors this guarded body. Match — identical to v83/v95. Guard on flag==0 is analyzer-flattened (tool limitation).

CScriptMan::OnAskSpeedQuiz#AskSpeedQuiz.notes: Dialog type 7 (AskSpeedQuiz). v87 CScriptMan::OnAskSpeedQuiz@0x792ba2 forwards to CWvsContext::OnInitialSpeedQuiz@0xabe8ba: Decode1(flag @0xabe8c9); when flag==0 (show): Decode4(type @0xabe916)+Decode4(answer @0xabe91f)+Decode4(correct @0xabe929)+Decode4(remain @0xabe933)+Decode4(remainSec @0xabe941). Atlas AskSpeedQuizConversationDetail mirrors this guarded body. Match — identical to v83/v95. Guard on flag==0 is analyzer-flattened (tool limitation).

CScriptMan::OnAskAvatar#AskAvatar.notes: Dialog type 8 (AskAvatar). v87 CScriptMan::OnAskAvatar@0x792330: DecodeStr(message @0x792351)+Decode1(count @0x792365)+loop count x Decode4(avatar look @0x792381). Atlas AskAvatarConversationDetail: AsciiString(Message)+Byte(len)+loop Int. Count is a single byte (Decode1). Match — identical to v83/v95. Analyzer flattens the loop.

calls.comment: avatar look id -- loop body (count iterations; analyzer flattens)

CScriptMan::OnAskMembershopAvatar#AskMem.notes: Dialog type 9 (AskMemberShopAvatar). v87 CScriptMan::OnAskMembershopAvatar@0x7924cc: DecodeStr(message @0x7924ed)+Decode1(count @0x792501)+loop count x Decode4(avatar look @0x79251d). Atlas AskMemberShopAvatarConversationDetail: AsciiString(Message)+Byte(len)+loop Int. Count is a single byte (Decode1) NOT int — Phase-2 unconditional byte-count fix VERIFIED correct for v87. Match — identical to v83/v95. Analyzer flattens the loop.

calls.comment: avatar look id -- loop body (count iterations; analyzer flattens)

CScriptMan::OnAskPet#AskPet.notes: Dialog type 0xA (AskPet). v87 CScriptMan::OnAskPet@0x792663: DecodeStr(message @0x79267f)+Decode1(pet count @0x792699)+loop count x (DecodeBuffer(8) cashItemSN @0x7926e7 + Decode1 @0x7926ef). The client DecodeBuffer(8) corresponds to atlas WriteLong(cashItemSN) (8-byte serial). Atlas AskPetConversationDetail: AsciiString(Message)+Byte(len)+loop (Long(SN)+Byte). Match — identical to v83/v95. Analyzer flattens the loop (one iteration modeled; tool limitation).

calls.comment: cashItemSN (8-byte SN; client DecodeBuffer(8), atlas WriteLong) -- loop body

CScriptMan::OnAskPetAll#AskPetAll.notes: Dialog type 0xB (AskPetAll). v87 CScriptMan::OnAskPetAll@0x7928f1: DecodeStr(message @0x79290b)+Decode1(pet count @0x792922)+Decode1(bExceptionExist @0x792931)+loop count x (DecodeBuffer(8) cashItemSN @0x792978 + Decode1 @0x792980). Atlas AskPetAllConversationDetail: AsciiString(Message)+Byte(len)+Byte(exception)+loop (Long(SN)+Byte). Match — identical to v83/v95. Analyzer flattens the loop.

calls.comment: cashItemSN (8-byte SN; client DecodeBuffer(8), atlas WriteLong) -- loop body

CScriptMan::OnAskBoxText#AskBoxText.notes: Dialog type 0xE (AskBoxText). v87 CScriptMan::OnAskBoxText@0x791e79: DecodeStr(message @0x791e94)+DecodeStr(default @0x791ea4)+Decode2(col @0x791eb9)+Decode2(line @0x791ecb). Atlas AskBoxTextConversationDetail: AsciiString(Message)+AsciiString(Default)+Int16(Column)+Int16(Line). Match — identical to v83/v95.

CScriptMan::OnAskSlideMenu#AskSlideMenu.notes: Dialog type 0xF (AskSlideMenu). v87 CScriptMan::OnAskSlideMenu@0x792bb4 forwards to CSlideMenuDlgEX ctor@0x7b90f3 -> SetSlideMenuDlg@0x7b92d1: Decode4(slideDlgType @0x7b92ee)+Decode4(menuType @0x7b92fc)+DecodeStr(button info @0x7b9303). Atlas AskSlideMenuConversationDetail: Int(Unknown)+Int(menuType)+AsciiString. Match — identical to v83/v95.

CUserLocal::TalkToNpc.notes: NPC_START_CONVERSATION. v87 CUserLocal::TalkToNpc@0x9e3066 (non-quest path): COutPacket(0x3D)+Encode4(npcObjectId @0x9e30bc)+Encode2(x @0x9e30d7)+Encode2(y @0x9e30ef). Atlas StartConversation writes Int(oId)+Int16(x)+Int16(y). Match — identical to v83/v95. (Quest path branches to CNpc::ShowQuestList, no packet.)

CScriptMan::OnSay#Reply.notes: NPC_CONTINUE_CONVERSATION (Say reply). Built inside v87 CScriptMan::OnSay@0x791828 after DoModal: COutPacket(0x3F)+Encode1(0 msgType @0x791938)+Encode1(action @0x79195a; -1/0/1 from prev/end/next). The msgType+action bytes belong to atlas ContinueConversation; the Say reply carries NO trailing field. Match — identical to v83/v95.

calls.comment: action (-1 prev / 0 end / 1 next; atlas ContinueConversation action)

CScriptMan::OnAskMenu#Selection.notes: NPC_CONTINUE_CONVERSATION AskMenu reply trailing field, built inside v87 CScriptMan::OnAskMenu@0x7921a8 after DoModal: COutPacket(0x3F)+Encode1(5 msgType @0x792295)+Encode1(action @0x7922ab/0x7922b7); when selected appends Encode4(selection @0x7922c2). The msgType+action bytes belong to atlas ContinueConversation; ContinueConversationSelection writes ONLY the trailing selection. Atlas Decode picks width at runtime (Available()>=4 -> Int32 wide else Byte). AskAvatar/AskMembershopAvatar (msgType 8/9) instead append Encode1 (1-byte response), so both client widths exist; modeled as the wide (Encode4) path. Analyzer flattens the branch — tool limitation. Match — identical to v83/v95.

calls.comment: selection / m_nSelect (AskMenu = 4-byte int; atlas ContinueConversationSelection wide path)

CScriptMan::OnAskText#Reply.notes: NPC_CONTINUE_CONVERSATION AskText reply, built inside v87 CScriptMan::OnAskText@0x791cd0 after DoModal: COutPacket(0x3F)+Encode1(3 msgType @0x791de7)+Encode1(accepted @0x791df5/0x791e1e); when accepted appends EncodeStr(text @0x791e13). The msgType+accepted bytes belong to atlas ContinueConversation; ContinueConversationText writes ONLY the trailing string. Match — identical to v83/v95. (AskNumber/AskBoxText follow the same accepted+payload shape with Encode4 / EncodeStr respectively.)

calls.comment: entered text (atlas ContinueConversationText trailing string; only when accepted)

CShopDlg::SetShopDlg.notes: OPEN_NPC_SHOP (NpcShopList). v87 CShopDlg::OnPacket@0x7a290d (nType==0x142) -> CShopDlg::SetShopDlg@0x79e4d0. Decode4(npcTemplateId @0x79e4f4)+Decode2(count @0x79e4fa)+per-commodity loop (while idx<count, no cap). Per item, the EXACT v87 decode sequence is (0x79e527..0x79e5b1): Decode4 itemId + Decode4 mesoPrice + Decode1 discountRate + Decode4 (token field; IDA names it nTokenItemID) + Decode4 period + Decode4 levelLimit + (itemId/10000 in {207,233} ? DecodeBuffer(8) unitPrice double : Decode2 quantity) + Decode2 slotMax. KEY CROSS-VERSION FINDING: v87 carries discountRate(byte)+ONE token int (3 ints total after meso: token/period/levelLimit). v83 had NEITHER discountRate NOR any token int (2 ints after meso). v95 has discountRate + TWO token ints (tokenItemId + tokenPrice = 4 ints after meso). Atlas ShopList.Encode gates WriteByte(DiscountRate) on GMS>=87 and WriteInt(TokenTemplateId) on GMS>=95, then unconditionally writes TokenPrice/Period/LevelLimit. For v87 atlas emits: meso + discountRate(byte) + [no TokenTemplateId, <95] + TokenPrice(int) + Period(int) + LevelLimit(int) = byte + 3 ints — wire layout MATCHES the v87 client byte+3-int read exactly (the single v87 token int is filled by atlas TokenPrice). VERIFIED correct for v87, no atlas change. Analyzer flattens the loop (TOOL LIMITATION, manually verified). Export models templateId+count+one representative non-ammo item.

calls.comment: commodity count (loop bound; no max cap)
[item] discountRate (nDiscountRate; GMS>=87, present in v87 @0x79e54e; absent in v83)
[item] token field (single int @0x79e558; IDA labels nTokenItemID; atlas TokenPrice fills this slot for v87 — v95 splits into tokenItemId+tokenPrice, v83 has none)
[item] quantity (non-ammo branch; ammo categories 207/233 read DecodeBuffer(8) unitPrice double instead)

CShopDlg::OnPacket#Simple.notes: CONFIRM_SHOP_TRANSACTION (NpcShopOperationSimple). v87 CShopDlg::OnPacket@0x7a290d (nType==0x143) switch(Decode1 mode @0x7a29ae). Mode-only arms (cases 0,1,2,3,4,5,8,9,10,16 and default) read no further packet fields — they show a StringPool Notice. Atlas ShopOperationSimple writes Byte(mode) only. v87 mode-enum values: cases 0xE/0xF append Decode4 (level-requirement/generic-error variants), case 0x11 reads Decode1+DecodeStr (string error). The mode byte is the server-config discriminator, not encoded by atlas beyond the leading byte. Match — family same as v83/v95. OP-FAMILY deferred to _pending.md.

CShopDlg::OnPacket#LevelRequirement.notes: CONFIRM_SHOP_TRANSACTION (NpcShopOperationLevelRequirement). v87 CShopDlg::OnPacket@0x7a290d case 0xE (@0x7a2aef) / 0xF (@0x7a2b6b): Decode1(mode)+Decode4(level/amount @0x7a2af3 / 0x7a2b6f) used to format a StringPool Notice. Atlas ShopOperationLevelRequirement writes Byte(mode)+Int(level). Match — family same as v83/v95.

CShopDlg::OnPacket#GenericError.notes: CONFIRM_SHOP_TRANSACTION (NpcShopOperationGenericError). v87 CShopDlg::OnPacket@0x7a290d case 0x11 (@0x7a2a89): Decode1(mode)+Decode1(hasMessage @0x7a2a89); when set DecodeStr(message @0x7a2a91). Atlas ShopOperationGenericError writes Byte(mode)+Bool(hasMessage)+(hasMessage?AsciiString). Match — family same as v83/v95.

CShopDlg::OnPacket#ShopDispatch.notes: NPC_SHOP dispatch wrapper (atlas NpcShop / NPCShopHandle). The leading shop-request discriminator byte written at the head of opcode 0x40 by v87 CShopDlg::SendBuyRequest/SendSellRequest/SendRechargeRequest (Encode1 0/1/2 @0x7a24ab/0x7a2728/0x7a28b7). Atlas Shop.Encode (shop.go) writes a single Byte(op); the per-action bodies (ShopBuy/ShopSell/ShopRecharge) carry ONLY the trailing fields. Op values (0=BUY,1=SELL,2=RECHARGE) are runtime-config discriminators. Match — identical role to v83/v95.

CShopDlg::SendBuyRequest.notes: NPC_SHOP (Buy body). v87 CShopDlg::SendBuyRequest@0x7a1d49 final send (0x7a249e..): after the leading Encode1(0) action byte (modeled separately as NpcShop/ShopDispatch), the body is Encode2(slot @0x7a24cb)+Encode4(itemId @0x7a24db)+Encode2(quantity @0x7a24e6)+Encode4(discountPrice/unitPrice @0x7a2503). Atlas NpcShopBuy writes Int16(slot)+Int(itemId)+Int16(quantity)+Int(discountPrice) — the action byte is NOT part of ShopBuy. Match — identical to v83/v95.

CShopDlg::SendSellRequest.notes: NPC_SHOP (Sell body). v87 CShopDlg::SendSellRequest@0x7a256b final send (0x7a2718..): after the leading Encode1(1) action byte (modeled separately as NpcShop/ShopDispatch), the body is Encode2(inventory pos @0x7a2733)+Encode4(itemId @0x7a273e)+Encode2(quantity @0x7a2749). Atlas NpcShopSell writes Int16(slot)+Int(itemId)+Int16(quantity) — the action byte is NOT part of ShopSell. Match — identical to v83/v95.

CShopDlg::SendRechargeRequest.notes: NPC_SHOP (Recharge body). v87 CShopDlg::SendRechargeRequest@0x7a278f final send (0x7a28a9..): after the leading Encode1(2) action byte (modeled separately as NpcShop/ShopDispatch), the body is Encode2(inventory pos @0x7a28c0). Atlas NpcShopRecharge writes Int16(slot) — the action byte is NOT part of ShopRecharge. Match — identical to v83/v95.

calls.comment: nHP (v87 int16; v95 widened to int32)
nMHP (v87 int16; v95 widened to int32)
nMP (v87 int16; v95 widened to int32)
nMMP (v87 int16; v95 widened to int32)
bSecondaryStatChangedPoint flag (@0xab6f0b; mask 0x180008; ONE trailing byte only — v87 has no battle-recovery-info second byte)
IP address as raw uint32 (stored directly into sin_addr.s_addr — network byte order preserved)
port (host byte order; htons applied when building sockaddr)
nUIID (window mode byte; dispatched to CWvsContext::UI_Open)
bSet (direction/lock mode flag) — v87 reads ONLY 1 byte; tAfterLeaveDirectionMode int32 absent (gate >=90)
mode (switch dispatch; cases 1-4 = error codes — no additional fields)
mode (case 7 = OPEN_SHOP; client calls SendOpenShopRequest — no further reads)
mode (cases 9/10/11/15 — client shows fixed string-pool notice, no further reads)
success flag (if 0 return early; if 1 show chat-log success message)
channelId (v14 — 0xFE/0xFD/0xFF = error; otherwise shows YesNo warp dialog)
flag (if 0 return immediately; atlas always encodes 1)
sMsg — message string (only read when flag != 0)
packet length (raw socket read; majorVersion parsed inline)
gm byte (Encode1 based on TSecType GM check @0x4a7418)
length uint16 (Encode2 exception log byte count @0x4a7371)
